title: Aalagar
content: {{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image= |name=Aalagar |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{DB|species|aalagar|Aalagar}} |class=[[Craniopod]]{{DB|species|bith|Bith}} |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions= |lifespan= |origin=[[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|1=This one is definitely Bith, a recording method of an isolated island race, the Aalagar, that concocted the knotting style as a means of recording genealogies—'''''strings of ancestors.'''''|2=Dr. Rotham|3=''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''}} The '''Aalagar''' were a [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]] of the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]], a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]]. The Aalagar lived on remote islands, isolated from other Bith communities. The Aalagar developed a form of communication that involved tying knots of string to convey elaborate symbology. The [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady of the Sith]] [[Lumiya]] used a string of Aalagar symbology as part of a collection of [[Prophet/Legends|prophetic]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lorrd Artifact|tassels]] that she used to attract [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], whom she later turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. ==Biology and appearance== The Aalagar were one of the [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]] of the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]]—others included the [[Y'bith/Legends|Y'bith]]{{DB|species|ybith|Y'bith}}—a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of highly advanced [[craniopod]]s. ==Society and culture== The Aalagar developed a form of knot-tying in which they recorded their genealogy—literally "strings" of ancestors. This eventually evolved into a more complex form of communication, capable of conveying recorded thoughts and statements. ==History== {{Quote|He will ruin those who deny justice.|An Aalagar knot string|}} [[File:ClakdorVII-NEGAS.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Clak'dor VII, the Aalagar homeworld]] The Aalagar lived on remote islands on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]], located in the [[Mayagil sector/Legends|Mayagil sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} As the result of a Bith [[Nozho–Weogar War/Legends|civil war]] centuries prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was transformed into a dangerous wasteland. The Aalagar lived in isolation from the rest of the Bith species. Prior to the events of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady of the Sith]] [[Lumiya]] used an Aalagar knot string as part of a series of [[Lorrd Artifact|tassels]] containing [[Prophet/Legends|prophecies]] about [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]]. The Aalagar strand contained the message "He will ruin those who deny justice." Lumiya left the series of tassels aboard [[Toryaz Station]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], trusting Solo would be attracted to the artifact, and in the process of discovering its meaning be led to her. Solo eventually obtained a translation of the Aalagar symbology from [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Heilan Rotham]] of [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]]. Knowing the tassels would bring Solo to Lorrd, Lumiya waited for him on the planet, and made contact with him shortly after his arrival, beginning a relationship that would eventually lead him to become the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Caedus.''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The Aalagar were first mentioned in the [[2006]] novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'', which was written by [[Aaron Allston]] and the first book in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series. ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' {{1stm}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|species|aalagar|Aalagar}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Aalagar |es=Aalagar |deGA=1 }} [[Category:Bith subspecies]]
